headline: BREAKING: US B-2 bombers launch second wave of strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities
summary: Pentagon confirms B-2 Spirit stealth bombers from Whiteman AFB have commenced a second round of precision strikes targeting Iran's Fordow enrichment complex. Initial battle damage assessment indicates significant destruction of centrifuge halls.

headline: Iranian ballistic missiles strike Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar — casualties reported
summary: Multiple Shahab-3 and Fateh-110 missiles hit the perimeter of Al Udeid Air Base, the largest US military facility in the Middle East. CENTCOM reports activating Patriot defense systems. At least 3 missiles penetrated outer defenses. Casualty numbers unconfirmed.

headline: Dubai Mall bombing death toll rises to 58 as rescuers pull survivors from rubble
summary: Emergency services in Dubai continue search and rescue operations at the Dubai Mall following yesterday's attack. UAE Interior Ministry confirms 58 dead, 200+ injured. Houthi-linked group claims responsibility citing UAE support for US operations.

headline: CENTCOM FLASH: USS Eisenhower carrier group engages Iranian fast-attack boats in Strait of Hormuz
summary: Multiple Iranian IRGC-N fast-attack craft attempted swarming approach on USS Eisenhower CSG. F/A-18E Super Hornets and escort destroyers engaged. 4 Iranian boats destroyed, 2 retreated. No US casualties. Strait of Hormuz traffic suspended.

headline: UN Security Council emergency session — Russia and China block ceasefire resolution
summary: Russia and China have vetoed a US-backed ceasefire resolution at the UN Security Council. Russian Ambassador Nebenzia called the US strikes 'unprovoked aggression.' China's representative demanded immediate withdrawal of all US forces from the Persian Gulf.

headline: Brent crude surges past $148/barrel — highest since 2008 crisis
summary: Global oil prices continue their explosive rally as the Strait of Hormuz effectively closes to commercial traffic. Brent crude hit $148.67, WTI at $143.22. Saudi Aramco activating emergency reserves. US Strategic Petroleum Reserve release authorized by executive order.

headline: Major cyberattack hits US East Coast power grid — 12 million without power
summary: DHS CISA confirms Iranian-linked APT group 'Charming Kitten' has successfully compromised SCADA systems across 3 major East Coast utility providers. Rolling blackouts affecting New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut. Emergency generators activated at critical facilities.

headline: Navy SEALs capture Iranian Revolutionary Guard minelayer vessel in Gulf of Oman
summary: US Navy SEAL Team Six operatives successfully boarded and seized an IRGC Navy vessel caught deploying naval mines in Gulf of Oman shipping lanes. 23 IRGC sailors detained. Recovered intelligence materials being evaluated by ONI.

headline: Japan suspends all Iranian oil imports effective immediately — joins US sanctions coalition
summary: Japanese PM announces immediate suspension of remaining Iranian oil imports and freezing of $2.1B in Iranian assets held in Japanese banks. Japan joins expanded sanctions coalition alongside EU, Australia, and South Korea.

headline: Unverified: Satellite imagery shows activity at Iranian Parchin military complex
summary: Multiple OSINT channels sharing what appear to be commercial satellite images showing vehicle movement and possible launch preparations at Iran's Parchin military complex. Pentagon has not confirmed. Could indicate medium-range ballistic missile preparation.

headline: Global markets in freefall — Dow plunges 1,800 points, circuit breakers triggered
summary: Major US indices in steep decline as conflict escalation fears grip markets. Dow Jones down 1,847 pts (-5.2%), S&P 500 down 6.1%, NASDAQ down 7.3%. NYSE Level 1 circuit breaker triggered, halting trading for 15 minutes. Gold surges past $2,900/oz.

headline: Hezbollah declares 'full solidarity' with Iran — launches 150+ rockets into northern Israel
summary: Hassan Nasrallah's successor issues video statement declaring Hezbollah will 'open the northern front' in support of Iran. IDF reports 150+ rockets launched from southern Lebanon targeting Haifa, Safed, and military installations. Iron Dome intercepts majority but several impact reported.

headline: Tehran anti-war protests erupt — thousands defy regime in Azadi Square
summary: Massive anti-war demonstrations in Tehran's Azadi Square with estimates of 50,000+ protesters demanding an end to the conflict. IRGC and Basij militia deployed with water cannons and tear gas. Social media blackout imposed but Starlink terminals smuggled in maintain partial connectivity.

headline: Pentagon activates THAAD missile defense batteries across Gulf states
summary: DoD announces deployment of additional Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) batteries to Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Bahrain. Systems designed to intercept Iranian medium-range ballistic missiles. B-52H bombers redeployed from Diego Garcia to Al Udeid.

headline: China's PLA Navy deploys Type 055 destroyer group to Indian Ocean — Beijing warns against escalation
summary: Chinese Foreign Ministry confirms deployment of PLA Navy Surface Action Group including Type 055 cruiser Nanchang to northern Indian Ocean. Beijing states mission is to 'protect Chinese commercial interests and nationals.' Pentagon monitoring movement closely.

headline: Riyadh residential district hit by ballistic missile — at least 67 confirmed dead
summary: Houthi-launched ballistic missile strikes residential neighborhood in southern Riyadh. Saudi Civil Defense reports 67 dead, 150+ wounded. Missile evaded Saudi Patriot defense systems. International community condemns attack on civilian infrastructure.

headline: IDF launches ground incursion into southern Lebanon — Operation Northern Shield
summary: Israeli Defense Forces announce large-scale ground operation into southern Lebanon following Hezbollah rocket barrage. Merkava IV tanks and Namer APCs crossing the border near Metula. IDF Chief of Staff states objective is to create 10km buffer zone.

headline: Russia places strategic forces on heightened alert — Kremlin warns of 'unpredictable consequences'
summary: Russian Defense Ministry announces heightened readiness status for strategic deterrence forces. Kremlin spokesperson Peskov warns that US strikes on Iran could have 'unpredictable and catastrophic consequences for global security.' NATO Secretary General calls for de-escalation.

headline: IRGC claims downing of US MQ-9 Reaper drone over Bandar Abbas
summary: IRGC-affiliated channels claim to have downed a US Air Force MQ-9 Reaper surveillance drone using a 3rd Khordad air defense system near Bandar Abbas naval base. Video purportedly showing wreckage circulating. Pentagon has not confirmed loss of any aircraft.

headline: Lloyd's of London suspends all marine insurance for Persian Gulf transit
summary: Lloyd's of London and major reinsurers suspend marine war risk coverage for vessels transiting the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz. Global shipping companies rerouting around Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 days to Asia-Europe routes. Container freight rates triple overnight.

headline: Iraqi PMF militias launch drone attacks on US forces at Ain al-Asad air base
summary: Iranian-backed Popular Mobilization Forces launch coordinated drone and rocket attacks on Ain al-Asad air base in western Iraq. C-RAM systems engage multiple incoming threats. 2 US soldiers wounded. Pentagon considering retaliatory strikes against PMF positions.

headline: SIGINT: Multiple US KC-135 tankers orbiting over eastern Mediterranean — surge refueling ops
summary: Flight tracking shows at least 8 KC-135 Stratotankers and 3 KC-46 Pegasus aircraft establishing refueling orbits over eastern Mediterranean and northern Saudi Arabia. Pattern consistent with sustained combat air operations. B-1B Lancer bombers also detected departing RAF Fairford, UK.

headline: US State Department orders non-essential embassy personnel evacuation from 6 Middle East posts
summary: Secretary of State orders authorized departure of non-essential personnel and families from US embassies in Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, UAE, Jordan, and Lebanon. Travel advisory raised to Level 4 (Do Not Travel) for all Gulf states. Commercial airlines suspending flights to region.

headline: Analysis: Iran's missile arsenal — 3,000+ ballistic missiles capable of reaching US bases
summary: Jane's Intelligence Review estimates Iran maintains approximately 3,000 operational ballistic missiles including Shahab-3, Emad, Khorramshahr, and Sejjil variants. Range covers all US bases in the Middle East. Underground missile cities' hardened against bunker busters.

headline: Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei addresses nation: 'Divine victory is assured'
summary: Ayatollah Khamenei delivers televised address calling US strikes 'the greatest strategic mistake in American history.' Vows 'crushing response at the time and place of our choosing.' Orders full IRGC mobilization. Iranian Foreign Minister recalls ambassadors from 12 countries.

headline: NORAD raises continental defense readiness to DEFCON 3 amid global escalation
summary: North American Aerospace Defense Command confirms DEFCON 3 posture activated for continental US defense. Additional F-22 Raptor squadrons deployed to forward operating locations. AWACS aircraft maintaining 24/7 barrier patrols. SecDef describes as 'prudent precaution.'

headline: Reports of GPS spoofing affecting commercial aviation across entire Middle East region
summary: Multiple airline pilots reporting GPS navigation anomalies and spoofing across Gulf airspace. UAE GCAA and Saudi GACA issue NOTAMs warning of unreliable satellite navigation. Suspected Iranian electronic warfare operation targeting GPS constellation signals in theater.

headline: UNHCR: 200,000+ refugees fleeing Iraq and Lebanon amid regional conflagration
summary: UN Refugee Agency reports massive displacement as fighting spreads across Iraq, Lebanon, and Gulf states. Jordan and Turkey opening emergency refugee camps. UNHCR appeals for $4.2B in emergency funding. WHO deploying surgical teams to field hospitals across region.

headline: White House considering invoking War Powers Act — Congressional leaders briefed
summary: Senior administration officials have briefed congressional Gang of Eight on military operations. House Speaker demands full War Powers Act compliance within 48 hours. Senate Armed Services Committee scheduling emergency classified hearing. Bipartisan support fracturing.

headline: Sentinel-2 imagery confirms destruction at Natanz nuclear facility — 3 buildings leveled
summary: Commercial satellite imagery from ESA Sentinel-2 confirms significant damage at Iran's Natanz uranium enrichment facility. Three above-ground buildings completely destroyed. Possible secondary explosions observed. Underground centrifuge halls' structural integrity unknown — requires SAR analysis.

headline: FLASH: Iran fires anti-ship cruise missiles at US destroyer USS Carney in Gulf of Oman
summary: USS Carney (DDG-64) engaged by 2 Iranian Noor anti-ship cruise missiles while operating in Gulf of Oman. Ship's Aegis combat system and CIWS successfully intercepted both missiles. No damage or casualties reported. USS Carney returning fire with Tomahawk cruise missiles against coastal launcher sites.

headline: EU activates crisis response mechanism — Borrell calls for 'immediate humanitarian corridors'
summary: European Union's Political and Security Committee activates its Integrated Political Crisis Response (IPCR) mechanism. High Representative Borrell demands humanitarian corridors for civilian evacuation. France and UK deploying naval assets to eastern Mediterranean for evacuation operations.

headline: CrowdStrike: Iranian APT33 targeting US defense contractors with zero-day exploits
summary: Cybersecurity firm CrowdStrike identifies active campaign by Iranian state-sponsored group APT33 (Elfin) targeting US defense contractors including Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, and Northrop Grumman. Exploiting previously unknown Windows kernel vulnerability. CISA issues emergency directive.

headline: China halts rare earth exports to US citing 'national security' — chip production threatened
summary: Beijing announces immediate suspension of rare earth mineral exports to the United States in apparent retaliation for US Iran operations. Export controls cover 17 rare earth elements critical for semiconductor, missile guidance, and EV production. Industry analysts warn of severe supply chain disruption.

headline: Gas prices surge to $6.40/gallon average — DOE activating emergency fuel reserves
summary: National average gasoline price hits $6.40/gallon, up $1.85 in 48 hours. Department of Energy authorizes emergency release from Strategic Petroleum Reserve. President invokes Defense Production Act for domestic refinery output. California stations reporting $8.20/gallon.
